# Overiew
itplz helps to add the appropriate ITP files to topologies as well as identify any issues. It can be helpful when working with topology files generated by INSANE.t

## Commands
### `itplz print-includes`
This tool will print a set of include statements to STDOUT based on the set of desired molecule types and the directory or files containing the ITP files containing their definitions.
```
$ itplz print-includes --directories martini2 --moltypes W NA CL CHOL POPS

; Inlcudes generated by itplz
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_ions_v1.itp" ; NA,CL
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp" ; W
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_sterols_v1.itp" ; CHOL
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PS_v2.itp" ; POPS
```

### `itplz add-includes`
This tool is similar to `print-includes` but instead takes a rudimentary topology file as an argument. It adds any includes for molecules which have missing molecule type definitions.
```
$ cat topol.top

#include "martini.itp"
#include "martini/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PI_v2.itp"

[ system ]
; name
Protein in INSANE! 

[ molecules ]
; name  number
system          1
POPC           200 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PLPC           140 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PAPE           153 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
POPE           178 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
POPI            63 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PAPS           140 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
CHOL           370 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
W           171692
NA            2001
CL            1780

$ itplz add-includes --top topol.top --directories martini2 --files system.itp

#include "martini.itp"
#include "martini/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PI_v2.itp"
; added by itplz
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_ions_v1.itp" ; NA,CL
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp" ; W
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_sterols_v1.itp" ; CHOL
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PE_v2.itp" ; POPE,PAPE
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PS_v2.itp" ; PAPS
#include "martini2/martini_v3.0.0_phospholipids_PC_v2.itp" ; POPC,PLPC
#include "system.itp" ; system

[ system ]
; name
Protein in INSANE! 

[ molecules ]
; name  number
system          1
POPC           200 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PLPC           140 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PAPE           153 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
POPE           178 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
POPI            63 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
PAPS           140 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
CHOL           370 ; Defined in the packaged 'lipids.dat'.
W           171692
NA            2001
CL            1780
```



### `itplz check-top`
Reports any issues with the topology. Given a set of additional files, it can also say whether or not they can be included to resolve molecule types.

### `itplz moltypes`
Lists all the molecule types defined in the input itp file set

```
$ itplz moltypes --directories martini2/ --file system.itp

system    system.itp:18
W         martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:12
DMSO      martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:22
ACN       martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:37
HEX       martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:49
OCT       martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:64
DOD       martini2/martini_v3.0.0_solvents_v1.itp:79
....

```
